UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and general settings within the FynWealth application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, primarily for identification and communication purposes (external authentication is assumed).

preferredCurrency.description: The user's preferred currency for displaying financial information (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines categories for organizing bills and expenses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The descriptive name of the category (e.g., 'Groceries', 'Utilities', 'Rent').

description.description: A detailed description of what this category represents.

isSystemDefined.description: Flag indicating if this is a default, system-provided category.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created or customized this category. Null if it's a pure system-defined category. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Category)

iconName.description: A textual identifier or name for an icon associated with this category, for UI representation.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the category was last updated.

BillDefinition.title: BillDefinition
BillDefinition.description: Defines the recurring aspects and details of a bill, such as subscriptions, rent, or EMIs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BillDefinition entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this bill definition. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N BillDefinition)

name.description: A descriptive name for the recurring bill (e.g., 'Electricity Bill', 'Netflix Subscription', 'Mortgage EMI').

initialAmount.description: The typical or initial payment amount for this recurring bill.

currency.description: The currency of the bill amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this bill belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N BillDefinition)

frequency.description: The recurrence pattern of the bill (e.g., 'monthly', 'annually', 'weekly', 'one-time').

startDate.description: The initial date when the recurring bill commenced or is set to commence.

notes.description: Any additional descriptive notes pertaining to this bill definition.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the bill definition was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the bill definition was last updated.

BillPayment.title: BillPayment
BillPayment.description: Records individual instances of bill payments, tracking their due dates, paid status, and actual amounts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BillPayment entity.

billDefinitionId.description: Reference to the BillDefinition this payment instance is associated with. (Relationship: BillDefinition 1:N BillPayment)

amountPaid.description: The actual amount paid for this specific bill instance.

dueDate.description: The specific due date for this particular bill payment cycle.

paidDate.description: The date and time when the payment was actually made.

status.description: The current payment status of this bill instance (e.g., 'due', 'paid', 'overdue', 'scheduled').

notes.description: Any additional notes or details pertaining to this specific bill payment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this bill payment instance was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this bill payment instance was last updated.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Records individual spending transactions made by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who recorded this expense. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Expense)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the expense.

currency.description: The currency of the expense amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').

description.description: A brief textual description or purpose of the expense.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this expense falls under. (Relationship: Category 1:N Expense)

expenseDate.description: The date when the expense actually occurred.

captureMethod.description: The method used to record the expense (e.g., 'manual', 'scan', 'voice').

originalDocumentUrl.description: URL or path to the original scanned bill, invoice, or voice recording, if applicable.

notes.description: Any additional notes or contextual information about the expense.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the expense record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the expense record was last updated.

Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Enables users to set spending limits for specific categories over defined periods.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Budget entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this budget. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Budget)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this budget applies to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Budget)

periodType.description: The type of recurrence period for the budget (e.g., 'monthly', 'weekly', 'annually').

periodStart.description: The start date of the current budget period.

periodEnd.description: The end date of the current budget period.

amountLimit.description: The maximum spending amount allowed for the associated category within this budget period.

alertThresholdPercentage.description: An optional percentage (0-100) of the amountLimit at which the user should receive an alert.

isActive.description: Indicates whether this budget is currently active and being monitored.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the budget was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the budget was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access restricted to the owner, where `request.auth.uid` must match `userId` in the path.

Stores system-defined categories (e.g., 'Groceries', 'Utilities'). These are globally readable by all authenticated users. Documents in this collection will have `isSystemDefined: true` and `userId: null`.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the system-defined category.

definition.description: Stores user-defined categories. Private to the owning user. Access restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Documents in this collection will have `isSystemDefined: false` and include the `userId` field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this category.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user-defined category.

definition.description: Stores definitions for recurring bills (e.g., Netflix, Rent). Private to the owning user. Access restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Each document includes the `userId` field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this bill definition.

params.name: billDefinitionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the bill definition.

definition.description: Records individual instances of bill payments associated with a specific BillDefinition. Private to the owning user. Access restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`). CRITICAL: Each document *must* include the denormalized `userId` field (copied from its parent BillDefinition) to ensure authorization independence and avoid `get()` calls in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent bill definition and this bill payment.

params.name: billDefinitionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent bill definition.

params.name: billPaymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific bill payment instance.

definition.description: Records individual spending transactions made by a user. Private to the owning user. Access restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Each document includes the `userId` field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who recorded this expense.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the expense record.

definition.description: Enables users to set spending limits for specific categories over defined periods. Private to the owning user. Access restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Each document includes the `userId` field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who created this budget.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the budget.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for FynWealth is designed with security, scalability, and debuggability as core priorities, strictly adhering to the mandated design principles.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Strategy A):**
This structure primarily achieves authorization independence by embedding the `userId` directly within every document that requires user-specific ownership checks. For instance, `BillDefinition`, `Expense`, and `Budget` documents each contain a `userId` field. Crucially, for `BillPayment` documents, which are nested under `BillDefinition`s, the `userId` of the owning `UserProfile` is explicitly denormalized into each `BillPayment` document itself. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document ownership, thereby supporting atomic operations (transactions/batches) and preventing security rule failures or performance bottlenecks due to cascading reads. Rules can simply compare `request.auth.uid` with the `userId` field present in the document itself or derived from its direct path, ensuring atomicity and straightforward debugging.

**Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security Posture (Strategy B):**
Data is segregated based on its security posture. For example, `Category` entities are split into two distinct collections:
1.  `/system_categories/{categoryId}`: These are read-only for all authenticated users (and effectively immutable by users), containing `isSystemDefined: true` and `userId: null`. This provides a consistent security profile where no `userId` check is needed for read access.
2.  `/users/{userId}/categories/{categoryId}`: These are user-defined and private, containing `isSystemDefined: false` and `userId: {userId}`. Access is strictly controlled by the `userId` in the path, ensuring privacy.
Similarly, all other private, user-owned data (`UserProfile`, `BillDefinition`, `Expense`, `Budget`, `BillPayment`) resides under `/users/{userId}/...` paths, creating a homogeneous security posture within each segment and greatly simplifying rule logic.

**QAPs (Queries are not Filters) Support:**
By using structural segregation and embedding ownership (`userId`) directly into documents, `list` operations inherently respect security rules without relying on inefficient and insecure filtering. 
*   When a user queries `/users/{userId}/billDefinitions`, the security rules can enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` directly on the path, allowing a secure and performant list of *only* their own bill definitions.
*   Similarly, for `/users/{userId}/expenses` and `/users/{userId}/budgets`, `list` operations will only return documents owned by the authenticated user.
*   For `/system_categories`, `list` operations will return all system-defined categories, which are intended to be publicly readable by all authenticated users, requiring no filtering based on `userId`.
This design ensures that any query issued by a client will only return data that the client is explicitly authorized to access, making rules simple, robust, and debuggable.
